question.text:
Consider the following two statements: 

S1:  If a candidate is known to be corrupt, then he 
will not be elected. 

S2:  If a candidate is kind, he will be elected. 

Which one of the following statements follows from 
S1 and S2 as per sound inference rules of logic?

options.text: If a person is known to be corrupt, he is kind

If a person is not known to be corrupt, he is 
not kind

If a person is kind, he is not known to be 
corrupt

If a person is not kind, he is not known to be 
corrupt 

Propositional Logic

question.text:
Geetha has a conjecture about integers, which is of 
the form  

пАҐx(P(x) пГЮпА§yQ(x, y), 

where P is a statement about integers, and Q is a 
statement about pairs of integers. Which of the 
following (one or more) option(s) would imply 
Geetha's conjecture?

solution.text:
L : пАҐx[ P(x)вЖТ пА§y Q(x,y)] 

"For every x if P(x) is true then there exists 
some y such that Q(x,y) will be true. 

Option : 

(i)  пА§x(P(x) пГЩпАҐy Q(x,y)) 


"For some x. P(x) is true and for all y 

Q(x.y) is true which is not implies L" 

(ii) пАҐxпАҐyQ(x,y) 

"For every x and every yQ(x,y) is true 
which implies L" 

(iii) пА§y пАҐx[P(x)вЖТ Q(x,y)] 

"These exists some y such that for every x 
if P(x) is true then Q(x,y) is also true which 
implies L" 

(iv) пА§xP(x)пГЩ пА§yQ(x,y) 

"There exists some x for which P(x) is true 
and also for some y Q(x,y) is true which 
cannot implies L"
